summary: Iteration 17 — Homepage copywriting upgrade verification. Backend pytest 21/21 PASS in 2.82s. Homepage SEO confirmed: document.title='Kobi Israel | Photography, Moving Image & Limited Editions' (58 chars, ≤60 OK), meta[name=description] exact match (151 chars, ≤155 OK), og:title='Kobi Israel — Still & Moving Diaries' OK, og:description starts with 'An archive of photography, moving image, books and limited edition prints' OK. Concept heading 'A Chaos of Appearances' OK; concept body contains 'still and moving diaries from the unstable theatre of memory' (capitalized 'Still…' as it begins a sentence — case-insensitive match passes, spec literal is mid-sentence lowercase). Featured Project Cuba verbatim phrase 'The confusing and bewildering paradox of the masculine and militarist blended with the homoerotic in Cuba' rendered OK. Selected Works grid renders 7 cards with all required titles + year ranges (Cuba 1993–2012, River of Three Crossings 2001–2017, Fragments of Life 2000–2007, Intimate Strangers 2001–2006, Views 1999–2003, Parisian Postcards 2010 'Three-channel video installation, 18 min', Investigating Things Past 2023). Artist statement contains 'remembrance of things past' OK. Moving section mentions 'A Cuban Love Story', 'Parisian Postcards' and 'Investigating Things Past' OK. Hero image alt = exact spec string OK. Featured project image alt contains 'Image from Cuba, Love Story by Kobi Israel' OK. All 9 forbidden commercial phrases ABSENT OK. Both hero CTAs present and functional: [data-testid='hero-cta-selected-works'] scrolls to #selected-works (hash + element in viewport confirmed); [data-testid='hero-cta-collector'] click triggers scrollIntoView toward #contact. 15 regression routes (/, /still, /moving, /projects/cuba-love-story, /projects/parisian-postcards, /projects/investigating-things-past, /prints, /books, /archive, /cv, /journal, /contact, /migration-review, /launch-checklist, /legal/privacy, /not-a-real-page) all HTTP 200 with ZERO console errors. Mobile 390x844 on /: scrollWidth=clientWidth=390, no horizontal overflow. TWO content-threading misses found on project detail pages — see frontend_issues.

integration_issues.flow: ProjectDetail data threading — STILL_PROJECTS.medium and STILL_PROJECTS.description not reaching the project detail page
integration_issues.issue: On /projects/parisian-postcards the MEDIUM field renders 'TO BE CONFIRMED BY ARTIST' instead of the STILL_PROJECTS value 'Three-channel video installation, 18 min'. On /projects/investigating-things-past the MEDIUM field renders 'TO BE CONFIRMED BY ARTIST' instead of 'Still & moving images installation' AND the description on the page is the enrichment.intro_statement ('An autobiographical archive in which childhood, exile and recollection are continually re-read.') NOT the upgraded Wix description ('Seven 110mm negatives, separated by hand from a forgotten envelope — involuntary memories returning out of order, in their own light.'). Root cause: /app/frontend/src/lib/projects.js getProject() returns only {slug,title,subtitle,year_range,location,intro_statement,hero_image,hero_alt,hasStill,hasMoving,still,moving,gallery_images,selected_works,book,exhibition_history,publication_history,press_quotes} — it does NOT include `medium`, `format`, `status`, `number_of_works`, `related_moving_works`, `related_books`, `related_prints`, `related_texts`, `archive_status`, `availability_status`, or `last_updated`, yet /app/frontend/src/pages/ProjectDetail.jsx reads project.medium / project.format / project.status / project.number_of_works etc from the result. Result: undefined → ProjectDetailTemplate falls back to TBC placeholders. Also: enrichment.intro_statement overrides base.description, so even with medium fixed the Wix description text for Parisian Postcards and Investigating Things Past would not appear.

issues: Content drift between Hero.jsx and site.js HERO_COPY_OPTIONS.option_2: Hero.jsx hardcodes 'Photography, moving images and autobiographical archives of masculinity, desire, exile and memory.' (plural 'images') whereas site.js option_2.positioning says 'Photography, moving image and autobiographical archives of masculinity, desire, exile and memory.' (singular 'moving image'). Hero.jsx should read from HERO_COPY_OPTIONS rather than hardcoding so the recommended option stays the single source of truth.

action_items: Fix /app/frontend/src/lib/projects.js getProject() — extend the returned object to thread medium, format, status, description, and the related_* fields from STILL_PROJECTS / MOVING_WORKS into the template. Suggested return additions: medium: still?.medium ?? moving?.format ?? null, format: moving?.format ?? null, status: base.status, description: base.description ?? base.synopsis.

Decide enrichment.intro_statement priority for parisian-postcards and investigating-things-past — review request says descriptions on /projects/* should come from STILL_PROJECTS (verbatim Wix), so for these two slugs either remove the ENRICHMENT.intro_statement override or set intro_statement: base.description first and enrichment.intro_statement as fallback.

Sync Hero.jsx positioning copy with HERO_COPY_OPTIONS.option_2 — replace the hardcoded 'moving images' with 'moving image' (singular) OR refactor Hero.jsx to read HERO_COPY_OPTIONS.option_2.positioning so the constants stay the single source of truth.

Optional: review request literal for the testid was 'hero-cta-selected' but the actual constant is 'hero-cta-selected-works'. Both testIds resolve to working elements; just a doc-vs-code label drift to be aware of.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/frontend/src/lib/projects.js getProject() is the single bottleneck for project detail content — it currently drops half the STILL_PROJECTS schema (medium, status, tags, featured) before handing the object to ProjectDetail.jsx. ProjectDetail.jsx then assumes those fields exist (project.medium, project.format, project.status, project.number_of_works, project.related_*). This is a silent type contract mismatch that the template covers up with TBC placeholders. Should be unit-tested or simply spread base into the return: `return { ...base, slug, intro_statement: enrichment.intro_statement || base.description || base.synopsis, ... }`.

Hero.jsx hardcodes copy that also exists in HERO_COPY_OPTIONS.option_2 — when the artist swaps to option_1 or option_3 (per the comment in site.js) only the constants change, not the rendered text. The component should import HERO_COPY_OPTIONS and render from HERO_COPY_OPTIONS[HERO_COPY_OPTIONS.recommended].

site.js is now 1900+ lines — consider splitting into /app/frontend/src/data/site/{home,still,moving,prints,books,cv,archive,contact}.js with a barrel index so the file stays under the 700-line guideline.

ENRICHMENT in /app/frontend/src/lib/projects.js currently silently overrides upgraded copy from site.js — needs an explicit precedence comment or a per-field merge so future Wix copy updates are not silently masked.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on homepage assertions (SEO / content / forbidden-phrase / alt / 7-card grid / CTAs / mobile overflow / 15 regression routes zero console errors). 2 misses on /projects/parisian-postcards and /projects/investigating-things-past project-detail content threading — STILL_PROJECTS.medium and .description not reaching the template.

test_credentials: n/a — no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: None new; pytest run creates TEST_ prefixed inquiries/newsletter rows as before.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 17 homepage copy upgrade verified end-to-end. Backend 21/21. Homepage: SEO + 7-card grid + concept + featured + artist statement + moving copy + alt text + forbidden-phrase audit + hero CTAs + mobile overflow all PASS. The two regressions are both on /projects/<slug> detail pages, not the homepage: (a) MEDIUM field shows TBC placeholder instead of STILL_PROJECTS.medium (e.g. 'Three-channel video installation, 18 min' for Parisian Postcards is on the homepage card but NOT on the project page), and (b) the upgraded Wix description for Parisian Postcards and Investigating Things Past is overridden by ENRICHMENT.intro_statement in /app/frontend/src/lib/projects.js. Hero.jsx also hardcodes 'moving images' (plural) while HERO_COPY_OPTIONS.option_2 says 'moving image' (singular) — confirm which the artist wants. Actual hero testids are 'hero-cta-selected-works' (NOT 'hero-cta-selected') and 'hero-cta-collector'.
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE — content threading: /app/frontend/src/lib/projects.js getProject() at line 131 explicitly enumerates the fields it returns and drops `medium`, `format`, `status`, `tags`, `featured`, and all `related_*` arrays from STILL_PROJECTS / MOVING_WORKS before passing the object to /app/frontend/src/pages/ProjectDetail.jsx (which reads project.medium, project.format, etc at lines 53-63 → undefined). For description, enrichment.intro_statement is given precedence over base.description at line 137 → the Wix verbatim text for Parisian Postcards / Investigating Things Past is overwritten. REPRODUCTION: open /projects/parisian-postcards → MEDIUM field renders 'TO BE CONFIRMED BY ARTIST' instead of 'Three-channel video installation, 18 min'; open /projects/investigating-things-past → page does not contain '110mm negatives' or 'forgotten envelope'. MITIGATION: spread the base record into the return (`return { ...base, slug, ... }`) and invert the description precedence so STILL_PROJECTS.description wins unless ENRICHMENT explicitly opts in via a flag. Hero.jsx 'moving images' vs HERO_COPY_OPTIONS.option_2 'moving image' is a separate content drift — fix by importing the constant.
